lines.text: Feb. Tues 28, 195
Mr J. Edgar Hoover
director of F.B.I.
Washington D.C.
Dear Mr Hoover:- "Flying Saucers"
Your acknowledgement of my letter in
regard to your excellent & faithful service, rec'd Feb. 22.
I've had something "on my chest" for sev
years, and it may be the time and way to do something ab
it, now also. It was during a time of my seven years
Civil Service work here in Tulsa, while doing Engr. drafting
for the Corps of Engineers, War Department. At this time I be
to the matter serious thought.
For a number of intermittent nights at ab
the same time, I would hear a terrific very quick noise ov
the four unit apartment Bldg. at 44 East 17th St. where I h
a small 3 roomed apartment on the top floor. The noise onl
lasted a second or less, and always traveled north to south. It
at great speed this missel or whatever over head. that I could
never get to the window to look into the night sky. I reported it to
the F.B.I. here (local F.B.I.) & checked all the airports. no one t
of any craft having been there at that time. I clocked it after h
it 4 or 5 times, and as near as I can remember now it was ab
11:21 P.M. Then the time I again reported to the F.B.I. I had lon
before found it was not street nor building noise. The seco
time I called the F.B.I. the party at the other end, said wait a
moment,- soon he said, a Lt. of the force had been telling him
hearing the same thing a few blocks south of me at the 21st
bridge while out there on patrol duty!
Then a few week after those noises over head, ca
the first word of people, seeing "flying saucers".
Since living here at [REDACTED] late last s
a flying saucer must have been what I saw that looked like a
very round white cloud against a grey stormy sky (a light ga
overcast sky) It also rapidly drifted north to south and while
I wondered & looked at the (cloud?) sphere I saw it disintegrate a
something that looked like envelopes or paper fell toward th. ea.
